response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a web and full-stack engineer whose career centres on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in a supporting role and a bachelor-thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ that is mandatory for this position. He has no demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience, no Windows platform background, and no meaningful capital-markets, fixed-income, or investment-banking domain knowledge; a brief stint at a German retail investment startup does not constitute institutional trading-systems experience.
strengths: Polyglot engineering background spanning Java, TypeScript, DLang, and some C/C++, indicating adaptability to new languages.
Practical CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, CI pipelines) aligns with listed desirable skills.
Strong communication track record evidenced by public conference talks, multi-day workshops, and open-source community contributions.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months total, on Linux/embedded contexts) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows.
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL appears only as a generic line-item skill with no evidence of depth in financial-grade RDBMS environments.
Zero fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge; RIDE Capital is a retail investment app, not an institutional trading or banking system.

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth and platform mismatch
No capital markets or fixed-income domain exposure
